info.title: LLMPool Admin REST API
info.description:
RESTful management interface for LLMPool administration.
All upstreams require JWT authentication via the `x-admin-token` header.
All list upstreams support pagination via `page` and `page_size` query parameters.

AdminToken.name: x-admin-token
AdminToken.description: JWT token signed with the admin JWT secret configured in `llmpool.toml`.

provider.description: The provider type for this upstream

name.description: A display name for the upstream

api_key.description: The API key for the upstream

api_base.description: The base URL of the upstream

provider.description: The provider type for this upstream

name.description: The display name for the upstream

provider.description: The provider type for this upstream

input_token_price.description: Price per input token (decimal string, must not be negative)

output_token_price.description: Price per output token (decimal string, must not be negative)

name.description: The name for the new account

initial_credit.description:
Optional initial credit amount to add to the account's fund after creation.
If greater than zero, a credit balance change will be created and applied asynchronously.
Must not be negative.

label.description: Optional label describing the purpose of this API key

content.description: JSON object describing the balance change details

is_applied.description: Whether the balance change has been applied

account_id.description: The ID of the account to deposit to

amount.description: The deposit amount (must be positive, decimal string)

account_id.description: The ID of the account to withdraw from

amount.description: The withdrawal amount (must be positive, decimal string)

account_id.description: The ID of the account to credit

amount.description: The credit amount (must be positive, decimal string)

api_key.description: The API key for the upstream

api_base.description: The base URL of the upstream

input_token_price.description: Price per input token at the time of the event (decimal string)

output_token_price.description: Price per output token at the time of the event (decimal string)

event_data.description: JSON object containing the session event data

next_id.description: The ID of the last event in the current page. Use as `start` for the next request.

has_more.description: Whether there are more events after this page

get.description: Returns a paginated list of OpenAI upstreams.

post.description:
Creates a new OpenAI-compatible upstream by detecting its features and saving
the upstream along with its models to the database.

201.description: Upstream created successfully with detected models

502.description: Failed to detect upstream features from remote API

get.description: Returns a single upstream by its ID.

put.description: Updates an existing upstream. Only the provided fields will be updated.

get.description: Returns a single upstream by its name.
Returns the list of tags for a given upstream.

post.summary: Add a tag to an upstream
post.description: Adds a tag to the specified upstream. If the tag already exists, it is not duplicated.

200.description: Tag added (or already existed), returns updated tags

delete.summary: Remove a tag from an upstream
delete.description: Removes a tag from the specified upstream.

post.description:
Tests an OpenAI-compatible upstream by detecting its supported features.
This does NOT save anything to the database — it only probes the remote API.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of OpenAI models with optional filters.
Returns a single model by its ID.

put.description: Updates an existing model. Only the provided fields will be updated.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of accounts.

post.description:
Creates a new account. Optionally, an initial credit amount can be provided
which will be applied asynchronously to the account's fund.

400.description: Validation error (empty name or negative initial_credit)

409.description: An account with this name already exists

get.description: Returns a single account by their ID.

put.description: Updates an existing account. Only the provided fields will be updated.

409.description: An account with this name already exists

get.description: Returns a single account by their name.

Returns the fund (asset) information for a given account.
If the account has no fund record yet, returns a default fund with zero balances.

get.summary: List API keys for an account
get.description: Returns a paginated list of API keys for a given account.

post.summary: Create an API key for an account
post.description: Creates a new API key for the specified account.

get.summary: Get an API key by its key string
get.description: Returns the API credential info for the given apikey string.

delete.description:
Soft-deletes the API credential by setting `is_active = false`.
Also invalidates the Redis cache entry for this apikey.

post.description:
Creates a deposit for an account. This creates a BalanceChange record and enqueues
a task to apply it asynchronously. Deposits add to the account's cash balance.
The `unique_request_id` field is a client-provided idempotency key.

Creates a withdrawal for an account. This first checks that the account's fund has
sufficient cash (cash >= amount). If not, returns an error. Otherwise, creates
a BalanceChange record and enqueues a task to apply it asynchronously.
The `unique_request_id` field is a client-provided idempotency key.

400.description: Validation error (non-positive amount or insufficient funds)

post.description:
Creates a credit for an account. This creates a BalanceChange record and enqueues
a task to apply it asynchronously. Unlike deposits which add to the cash field,
credits add to the credit field of the account's fund.
The `unique_request_id` field is a client-provided idempotency key.

get.summary: Get a session event by ID
get.description: Returns a single session event by its ID.

Returns a cursor-paginated list of session events with optional session_id filter.
Use `start` and `count` for cursor-based pagination instead of `page` and `page_size`.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Cursor: event ID to start after (exclusive). Events with id > start are returned.

tags.name: Upstream Tests
tags.description: Test upstream feature detection without saving
